Risk_Title: High Defect Reopen Rate and QA Budget Overrun
Risk_Description: QA testing across all sprints shows medium defect leakage with multiple defects requiring 2-3 retesting cycles. Budget exceeded by ₹25,000 (8.3% overrun) due to increased retesting efforts. Defects reopened due to incomplete fixes, causing delivery delays and resource strain.
Potential_Impact: Reduced software reliability, increased cost, delayed delivery, customer dissatisfaction

Risk_Title: PR Approval Delays Blocking Development Velocity
Risk_Description: Consistent PR approval delays across all sprints causing cycle time increases. Developer raised PRs but faced delays in architecture review approvals, blocking bug fixes and feature implementations. Despite being under budget, velocity impact is significant.
Potential_Impact: Reduced development velocity, sprint goal misses, team frustration, cascading delays

Risk_Title: Environment Access and Production Dependency Bottlenecks
Risk_Description: Integration and production support tasks consistently blocked due to environment access permissions and production dependencies. DevOps budget exceeded by ₹20,000 (10% overrun) due to increased production monitoring needs. Environment readiness delays impacted UAT and production validation.
Potential_Impact: Production incidents, deployment delays, operational cost increase, SLA breach risk

Risk_Title: External Dependency and Access Permission Delays
Risk_Description: Spike tasks across all sprints show consistent blocking due to external dependencies, access permissions, and approval delays. Coordination efforts for UAT/prod access, environment readiness, and approvals caused timeline impacts. Week 6-8 showed Amber health status indicating escalating risk.
Potential_Impact: Timeline delays, blocked tasks, stakeholder escalations, project health degradation

Risk_Title: Production Monitoring Overhead and Incident Management Load
Risk_Description: Production support activities from Sprint 3 onwards show increased monitoring of production queues, exception alerts, and retry behavior. DevOps budget overrun indicates resource strain. Cutover rehearsal and hypercare phases (Sprint 7-8) added complexity with incident support requirements.
Potential_Impact: Operational cost increase, team burnout, incident response delays, production stability risk

Risk_Title: QA Automation Gap and Manual Testing Dependency
Risk_Description: Divya joined mid-sprint (Sprint 4) to improve QA automation coverage and defect revalidation. Despite addition, QA budget still exceeded due to manual testing dependency and regression turnaround time. Automation gap indicates technical debt and process inefficiency.
Potential_Impact: Extended testing cycles, manual effort dependency, scalability issues, quality risk

Risk_Title: UAT Signoff Delays and Compliance Evidence Gaps
Risk_Description: Sprint 6-8 focused on UAT support and compliance evidence preparation. Tasks blocked due to client UAT signoff delays and defect retest queue backlog. Compliance evidence gaps risk regulatory approval delays and go-live readiness.
Potential_Impact: Regulatory approval delays, go-live postponement, compliance penalties, audit findings

Risk_Title: Cutover Readiness and Hypercare Stabilization Risks
Risk_Description: Sprint 7-8 cutover rehearsal and hypercare phases show blocked tasks due to cutover checklist approval and interface freeze confirmation delays. Production monitoring increased, indicating stabilization challenges. Bala's planned leave and roll-off during critical hypercare phase adds resource risk.
Potential_Impact: Go-live failure, production incidents, rollback scenarios, customer impact, reputation damage

Risk_Title: Resource Over-Utilization and Burnout Risk
Risk_Description: Finance report shows actual resource utilization significantly exceeds planned allocation: Developers at 82% vs 70%, QA at 24% vs 20%, DevOps at 14% vs 10%. Higher workload due to fixes, retesting cycles, and production dependencies indicates resource strain and burnout risk.
Potential_Impact: Team burnout, attrition risk, quality degradation, productivity decline, morale impact

Risk_Title: Budget Variance and Category-Level Overruns
Risk_Description: Overall project shows minor positive variance of ₹5,000, but category-level analysis reveals QA (₹25,000 over) and DevOps (₹20,000 over) overruns offset by Development savings (₹40,000 under). Category overruns indicate process inefficiencies and risk of future budget breaches.
Potential_Impact: Budget breach risk, financial loss, stakeholder escalations, project funding constraints

Risk_Title: Defect Leakage Exceeding Acceptable Thresholds
Risk_Description: JIRA data shows medium defect leakage across sprints with reopen counts ranging from 0-2 per issue. Multiple defects required 2-3 retesting cycles indicating incomplete fixes and quality process gaps. Defect leakage exceeding 3% threshold poses reliability and customer satisfaction risks.
Potential_Impact: Customer dissatisfaction, production defects, reliability issues, warranty costs, reputation damage

Risk_Title: Leave Coverage Gaps During Critical Phases
Risk_Description: DSR data shows multiple leave instances (sick leave, planned leave, flexi holiday) across team members during critical sprint phases. While backup coverage was assigned, leave during Sprint 7-8 cutover and hypercare phases (e.g., Bala's planned leave on 24-Apr) adds risk to critical deliverables.
Potential_Impact: Knowledge gaps, handover delays, task ownership confusion, delivery continuity risk

Risk_Title: Project Health Degradation from Green to Amber
Risk_Description: WSR data shows project health status degraded from Green (Week 1-5) to Amber (Week 6-8), indicating escalating risks. PR approval delays, access dependencies, and production validations cited as key risk drivers. Amber status signals need for escalation and corrective actions.
Potential_Impact: Stakeholder confidence loss, escalation requirements, corrective action overhead, timeline pressure

Risk_Title: Integration Validation Delays Due to Environment Dependencies
Risk_Description: Integration tasks consistently show Resolved status but with remarks indicating dependency delays due to environment access. Data flow validation and consistency checks impacted by infra dependencies. Integration bottlenecks risk data integrity and system interoperability.
Potential_Impact: Data integrity issues, integration failures, system interoperability problems, production incidents

Risk_Title: Healthcare Data Security and HIPAA Compliance Risks
Risk_Description: Project involves healthcare integration with patient data handling. Access permission delays and environment dependency issues may indicate security control gaps. HIPAA compliance requires strict access controls, audit trails, and encryption. Any compliance breach risks regulatory penalties and reputation damage.
Potential_Impact: Regulatory penalties, legal liability, reputation damage, customer trust loss, audit findings

Risk_Title: Extended Regression Testing Turnaround Impacting Sprint Velocity
Risk_Description: Divya's addition in Sprint 4 aimed to improve regression turnaround time. However, QA budget overrun and continued In Testing status across sprints indicate extended regression cycles. Regression bottleneck impacts sprint velocity and go-live readiness.
Potential_Impact: Sprint velocity reduction, go-live delays, quality risk, team frustration, cascading impacts

Risk_Title: Hypercare Handover Risks During Team Roll-off
Risk_Description: Sprint 8 closing summary indicates Bala completed hypercare handover and was rolled off after final stabilization. Handover during critical hypercare phase risks knowledge gaps and support continuity. Production monitoring and incident response coverage transitioned to remaining team members.
Potential_Impact: Knowledge gaps, support continuity risk, incident response delays, production stability issues

Risk_Title: Positive ROI Despite Operational Challenges
Risk_Description: Finance report shows positive ROI of 128%, high cost efficiency, and high delivery value despite operational challenges. Strong delivery outcome aligned with business goals. However, category-level overruns and resource over-utilization indicate sustainability risks for future phases.
Potential_Impact: Future budget constraints, sustainability concerns, process improvement needs, optimization opportunities

Risk_Title: Cross-Functional Coordination Gaps Causing Dependency Delays
Risk_Description: Priya M's spike tasks focused on dependency tracking and cross-functional coordination. Consistent blocking due to approvals and access permissions indicates communication gaps between development, QA, DevOps, and external stakeholders. Coordination inefficiencies amplify dependency delays.
Potential_Impact: Dependency delays, stakeholder friction, escalation overhead, timeline impacts, team frustration

Risk_Title: Technical Debt Accumulation Due to Incomplete Fixes
Risk_Description: Multiple defects requiring 2-3 retesting cycles and reopen counts indicate incomplete fixes and technical debt accumulation. Quick fixes without root cause analysis risk future defects and maintenance overhead. Technical debt impacts long-term system maintainability and scalability.
Potential_Impact: Maintenance overhead, future defects, scalability issues, code quality degradation, refactoring costs
